title: Web search tool can return too much context
body:
It appears that the web search tool does not truncate the size of returned results which can cause unexpected errors.

The web search API appears to return up to 5 results, including effectively the full text content of each web page.  In some quick tests, it seems quite common to receive 50-100kB of data back, but sometimes results can much larger.  A search for the string "e" sometimes returns over 300kB of text.  At approximately 3 chars per token, this can still be over 100k tokens.

Octofriend currently dumps these giant search result text blobs directly into context.  On the next model chat request, it is extremely likely that a previous large search result will put the conversation context length over the model's context limit.  With automatic compaction at 90%, even if you derate the model's listed max context length by 50% you still risk exceeding the total token context limit after performing a web search tool call.

I'm not sure if there's a ton that Octofriend can do about this, it seems like maybe it's more a bug in the web search API than in Octofriend itself.

One potential Octofriend solution would be to take each web search text and result and send them to the model, with no other context, and ask the model to summarize it into a 2-3 paragraph summary, then inject just that summary for each search result into context.

title: Allow user to have some control over compaction
body:
Currently it looks like Octofriend's compaction triggers when the conversation history reaches 90% of the configured model's maximum context length and then summarizes all of the conversations since the last compaction (or if there was no previous compaction, since the start of the conversation).

It would be nice if the user had some control over this 90% size value as well as to specify how much of the existing conversation should go into the compaction.  For example, maybe I want to compact at 75% of the model's max context and I only want the oldest 50% of messages compacted.

Exposing this compaction interface to the user through a dedicated compaction menu that would show the currently configured settings as well as allow for the user to initiate a compaction event immediately (hence, ignoring the 90% trigger, just do it now, but either use the configured oldest percentage choice or let the user specify one).

Obviously letting the user easily see the current conversation length, measured in tokens, would be a great benefit to enabling the user to understand how best to use compaction, too.  Maybe showing the user the current conversation's context length goes in this menu, too?

title: Have a verbose mode to show TTFT and token generation rates
body:
It would be neat to be able to observe on each request within an interactive Octofriend execution if it could show similar metrics to what `octo bench tps` shows.  This might be helpful for people to better understand how their model provider is performing in a more real-world and real-time way.

This mode would default to being off and would need to be turned on via the menu, then when enabled after each request that Octo makes to the model provider it would print some stats out, similar to how tool calls give a quick summary in gray after the call completes.

title: Interactively launch with a pre-filled prompt
body:
I'm working on adding Octo support to [Sidecar's workspaces plugin](https://sidecar.haplab.com/docs/workspaces-plugin), and Sidecar allows the user to select a prompt/task for the agent to work on before launching it. On launch, the prompt gets put in the agent's message field. Claude Code gets launched in a way that auto-submits that pre-filled prompt, so it's effectively noninteractively launching an interactive session. Some way to accomplish something similar with Octo would be nice, but I'm not sure about the auto-submitting part. Leaving pressing enter up to the user would be fine with me.

If this is wanted in Octo, what UX should it have? There's already the `prompt` subcommand for using octo noninteractively. Should this pre-filling only happen when text is piped in? Should it be an optional positional argument when run with no subcommands? Both, something else? 🤔

title: Auto-allow tools are not directory scoped
body:
Octofriend uses a `SKIP_CONFIRMATION_TOOLS` list for tools that run without user confirmation:
- `read`, `list` - File and directory operations
- `fetch`, `skill`, `web-search` - Network/remote operations

Octofriend currently treats all filesystem access as equally safe, which is incorrect. Reading files within your project is fundamentally different from reading system files like:

- `~/.ssh/*` - SSH private keys
- `~/.aws/credentials` - AWS access keys  
- `/etc/passwd`, `/etc/shadow` - User account information
- Configuration files containing secrets and API tokens

Without project directory restrictions, an AI agent could:
- Read sensitive user credentials and secrets
- Gather system information for potential abuse
- Access unrelated projects and cross-contaminate data
- Follow malicious instructions that exploit unrestricted file access

The existing whitelisting system is tool-based (allowing/disallowing an entire tool type globally), not location-based.

Potential Solution:

File and directory tools should only skip confirmation when operating within the project directory, where octofriend was launched.

In-Project Access: Confirm automatically (current behavior)

Out-of-Project Access: Prompt the user:
- "Yes" - Allow this specific operation
- "Yes, and always allow [tool] in [directory]" - Whitelist this tool for this directory
- "No, and tell Octo what to do differently" - Reject the operation

Tools that don't access local files (`web-search`, `fetch`, `skill`) remain unchanged.

Concepts / Ideas for Implementation:

- Resolve paths to determine if they're within the project directory
- Extend whitelist to store `{tool, directory}` pairs (add project directory to list on startup)
- Whitelist covers the specified directory and its subdirectories

Refs: https://github.com/synthetic-lab/octofriend/issues/135

title: vim commands and motions code repeat themselves
body:
In the vim-mode handling of motions and commands, there's a large amount of duplicated code.  It would be nice if the commands could somehow leverage the motions directly instead of implementing effectively the same code over again.

This would provide one source of truth for each motion, whether it's from a user using it as a single key command or as a combination motion operation.

title: Add menu choice to modify an existing API key
body:
In https://github.com/synthetic-lab/octofriend/pull/131 we discovered that there's currently no menu way to modify an existing API key stored within keys.json5 file.  You sort of could do this before that PR by just adding another model from the same provider and giving a different API key, which would update that base URL's API key in keys.json5, but after that PR then we properly reuse existing known API keys for a given base URL.

It would be nice to have a Ctrl+p menu entry (probably in the "settings" section, with "Change the default model (c)" choice) to change an API key.  It would present each base URL to chose and if you pick one it would ask for the new API key and then save that to keys.json5 for you.  It wouldn't show the existing API keys.

title: Prettier pre-commit hook sometimes reflows unchanged code
body:
Currently there's a pre-commit hook that will run Prettier on files being committed.  This can lead to reflowing code which isn't actually a part of a given commit.  This makes it harder to review PRs as the actual code change can be burried within a bunch of unrelated formatting changes.

To reduce the impact of this, we should probably run Prettier on all the files in the project and make that be its own dedicated reformatting commit, so as to reduce the chances that normal commits for code changes end up having unrelated formatting fixes included in them.

Avoiding Prettier running requires using the `git commit --no-verify` option, which avoids running ALL the git commit hooks, even a user's local ones which are outside of octofriend itself.

title: vim-mode can place text left of the text input box
body: If typing and hitting enter while in vim insert mode at a VERY rapid pace, then text can end up being placed visually to the left of the left-most valid text input column: This appears to be the text wrapping incorrectly for newlines, as the text in columns 0 and 1 is actually at the end of the current line somehow. I do not hit this during normal typing, it's only when I really jam on the keyboard that I can make it happen. But probably some people can type coherently much faster than me so this should be fixed.

title: Allow user to see shell tool output
body:
I would like to have the ability to see the model's shell/bash tool output.  Currently it appears to be hidden pretty reliably although it seems that the models generally will paraphrase what the shell tool output told them.

I expect not everyone is going to want to see this, so maybe it should be configurable?

For example, each shell tool use here just tells me how many lines were output:

```
▶  Yeah, 517b675 seems reasonable to fixup against.  Go ahead

🐙 ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
   │The user has approved fixup to commit 517b675 ("Improve vim "d" commands").   │
   │Let me go ahead and commit the fix.                                           │
   ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯


shell: git add source/components/vim-mode.tsx && git commit --fixup 517b675
timeout: 5000
Got 17 lines of output

🐙 ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
   │The fixup has been committed. Let me verify the status.                       │
   ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
   Done. Here's the current commit history:



shell: git log --oneline -18
timeout: 5000
Got 19 lines of output

🐙 ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
   │All the fixups are in place. We now have:                                     │
   │                                                                              │
   │1. `9b9599b` - fixup! Create some vim multi-line utility functions (0 motion  │
   │fix)                                                                          │
   │2. `d4732fd` - fixup! Fix vim "0", "$", and "^" multi-line navigation (h      │
   │command fix)                                                                  │
   │3. `c6356ba` - fixup! Create some vim multi-line utility functions (getLineEnd│
   │ fix)                                                                         │
   │4. `aaec214` - fixup! Improve vim "d" commands (d0/d$ empty line fix)         │
   │5. `58a392d` - Fix vim escape from insert mode on empty lines (standalone)    │
   │                                                                              │
   │All set for the rebase when ready.                                            │
   ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
   All fixups are now in place:


   │ Commit    │ Target    │ Fix                                             │
   ├───────────┼───────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   │  9b9599b  │  34f8119  │ "0" motion calculates  lineStart                │
   │  d4732fd  │  4e873f7  │  h  command checks  lineStart                   │
   │  c6356ba  │  34f8119  │  getLineEnd  uses  Math.max(0, lineLength - 1)  │
   │  aaec214  │  517b675  │  d0 / d$  preserve cursor on empty lines        │

   Plus the standalone commit 58a392d for escape from insert mode.

   Ready for rebase!
```

title: Model context lengths do not consistently use "k" to mean 1024
body:
Anthropic uses "k" to mean 1000: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/context-windows

Hugging Face open weight models generally (but maybe not always) use "k" to mean 1024.

Because of this, instead of letting the user type in "200k" for the context length, we should have them type in the full number to ensure that we don't guess what "k" means incorrectly.  Configuring a model to have a longer context length than it actually has may cause unexpected results for users.

title: ACP (Agent Communication Protocol support)
body:
Hello folks!

Do you know if there are plans in supporting [ACP](https://agentcommunicationprotocol.dev/introduction/welcome) for `octofriend`? I'm currently using the Zed editor which gives the ability to integrate agents like `octofriend` as [external agents](https://zed.dev/docs/ai/external-agents) if they speak the ACP protocol.

It would be nice to be able to use `octofriend` in a more tightly-integrated IDE setting.

title: Octofriend version releases should be tagged
body:
It would be helpful if when Octofriend versions are released if they could be tagged in git.  There's no tags currently: https://github.com/synthetic-lab/octofriend/tags

This would help with tracking down errors that users report with released versions where the user gives the version number, like `0.0.52` from the currently released version.

title: Windows Compatibility Issue: Requires Bash Shell
body:
Description
After successfully installing octofriend via npm on Windows, the command fails to run in the default Windows shells (PowerShell and Command Prompt), requiring users to install additional software (Git Bash or WSL) to use the tool.
Steps to Reproduce

Install octofriend on Windows using npm: npm install -g octofriend
Attempt to run octofriend or octo in PowerShell or Command Prompt
Receive error message requiring bash shell installation

Expected Behavior
octofriend should run natively in PowerShell or Command Prompt after npm installation, similar to most other npm packages on Windows.
Actual Behavior
The command fails with the following error:
Windows Setup: Octo requires a bash shell to run. Install one of the following:
1. Git Bash Download: https://git-scm.com/downloads
2. WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) Setup: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install
Note: After installation, run Octo from Git Bash or WSL, not Command Prompt or PowerShell.
Environment

OS: Windows
Shell: PowerShell / Command Prompt
npm: Successfully installed the package
Node.js: (version not specified, but npm is working)

Impact
This creates an unexpected barrier to entry for Windows users:

Requires additional software installation (Git Bash or WSL)
Not mentioned in the installation documentation
Creates confusion since npm installation succeeds but execution fails
Inconsistent with typical npm package behavior on Windows

title: Does this work on Windows?
body:
I'm always a bit trepidacious about installing a new AI CLI on Windows as they rarely work as well as on Linux.

This is one thing that isn't mentioned in the Readme, and would be handy to know!

I know, everyone has a beef with you if you code on Windows but I honestly use it because my Linux installation tends to just body itself as soon as it opens the swapfile if I'm doing heavy work (compilation or even just browsing!)

EDIT: I'm going to try now and actually see.

title: Ability to resume conversations
body:
I understand privacy is part of octo's offering, but this seems like a relatively straightforward feature that can be implemented locally. This is deal breaker for me to be honest. I'll likely look into building it myself if I have the time. But this is a major part of my workflow. I often resume conversations especially when debugging, swap between them when working in a different context (problem) and that context is valuable so you never have to start from scratch. I guess it goes without saying that a compact/summarization feature is needed with saved conversations.

thinking pretty much same as Claude Code's /resume command (or place it in the menu).

Conversations save (maybe in json format) in the global local folder....and maybe have an option to change where that get's saved, though not critical.

title: WSL command line windows pop up on tool calls
body:
Every time octo tries to use tools several command line windows pop up. It doesnt break anything, its just really annoying.

I'm using Cursor on Windows 11.

title: Diffs are always huge / unfocused
body: Just trying octofriend out real quick, coming from OpenCode, because I really like Synthetic's API and I figured I'd try out your agent! It seems nice so far, but the way diffs are displayed bothers me: they're displayed as the *entire file* twice, side by side, to show the old and new versions; yes the changed lines are highlighted, but that's still a lot of extra information, when I'd prefer to just see a diff of the changed lines alone, with maybe a little context around them; it's also a *lot* of scrolling if your screen isn't very wide, because of the side-by-side view squeezing the already long full-file diffs. It'd be nice if diffs were displayed with interleaved lines or something.

title: `TypeError: Failed to parse URL from ,` when using model to analyze repository
body:
**Title:** `TypeError: Failed to parse URL from ,` when using model to analyze repository

**Description:**

When I asked the model to analyze a public repository, Octofriend started performing multiple `fetch` operations and eventually crashed with a `TypeError: Failed to parse URL from ,`.

**Environment:**

* OS: Linux
* Installation method: `npm install --global --omit=dev octofriend`
* Node: `v22.19.0`
* Octofriend version: `0.0.46`
* Model: `Kimi-K2-Thinking`

**Error Output:**

```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│     Now let me look at the build system and some key implementation files:   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

fetch: https://[REDACTED]/Makefile
Got 23 lines of output

╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│      Let me look at a few more files to understand the architecture better,  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

fetch: ,
010010 Waiting

node:internal/deps/undici/undici:13510
Error.captureStackTrace(err);
^

TypeError: Failed to parse URL from ,
at node:internal/deps/undici/undici:13510:13
at async Object.run (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/tools/tool-defs/fetch.js:25:30)
at async runTool (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/tools/index.js:14:12)
at async runTool (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/state.js:110:28) {
  [cause]: TypeError: Invalid URL
  at new URL (node:internal/url:825:25)
  at new Request (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:9586:25)
  at fetch (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:10315:25)
  at fetch (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:13508:10)
  at fetch (node:internal/bootstrap/web/exposed-window-or-worker:75:12)
  at Object.run (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/tools/tool-defs/fetch.js:25:36)
  at runTool (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/tools/index.js:14:22)
  at runTool (file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/state.js:110:34)
  at file:///~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/lib/node_modules/octofriend/dist/source/app.js:332:13
  ...
  code: 'ERR_INVALID_URL',
  input: ', '
}
```

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Install Octofriend globally using npm.
2. Run a session and ask the model to analyze a public GitHub repository (e.g., `octofriend analyze https://github.com/...`).
3. Observe that Octofriend starts fetching files, then fails with the error above.

title: add a command to start a new conversation without the need of restarting octo
Installation via homebrew
body:
Mind adding this to https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core so I can install via homebrew?

Thanks!

title: Basic Tool call error
body:
I was just trying out octofriend and hit an error on a fairly basic use case. I've installed octofriend and successfully added Claude Sonnet 4.5 as a model.

I started `octo` in an empty directory and provided the prompt below

```
Hey Octo we're going to create a new golang program in this directory. The Goal of this program is to connect to the local Docker socket, and list all the images available along with their size.
```

Octo's output was

```
   Hey Rory! That sounds like a fun project. Let me start by checking what's currently in this directory, then I'll create a Go program that connects to
   the Docker socket and lists all available images with their sizes.
```

Followed by the following error on the first tool call.

```
400 {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"messages.
                                      1.content.2.tool_use.input: Input should be a valid
                                      dictionary"},"request_id":"req_011CUAh2XLJWGB2jx2FNdpP7"}
```

title: Text/XML MCP resources
Can it work with my claude max subscription?
body: Hey can we use it with my claude max subscription and also does it have sub agents?

title: Error with tools
Confirm file change only once per session and per directory
body:
Currently although secure, we have to babysit the model with every single edit even within the same file. It would be good to:

- approve only once for a given file per session
- approve changes to a directory and its files only once per session.

title: Slash commands
body:
Hi! 

Thanks for a nice friend!

Just a curious question, what is the project philosophy: would incorporation of slash commands into octofriend be something that might be welcome as a contribution? I am thinking of:

/init (to create and update OCTO.md, perhaps with a customisable prompt)
/compact (well, as you know, to compact and continue work)

... or is the philosophy to keep all this in the menu that you can open via the Esc key?

My personal thinking is that the initialization as well as some form of agents would be necessary additions to many workflows. But of course, these are opinions.
